name: Multiple Virtual Security Devices Powered Off within Short Period of Time
notes:
This rule triggers when multiple virtual security devices (e.g virtual IDS, virtual SIEM component) are powered off in a short period of time.

Note: The Security Devices reference set must be populated with the relevant machine names or IDs.

If authorized users perform this action often, exclude them by adding in a rule condition.
Please see "Abnormal Number of Modifications Made on Virtual Machines" for an example.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Cloud, BB:DeviceDefinition: Virtualization
when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Virtual Machine Stopped
when any of Machine Identifier (custom) are contained in any of Security Devices - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when at least 3 events are seen with the same Event Name, Username and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 45 minutes
thresholds: when at least 3 events are seen with the same Event Name, Username and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 45 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Multiple Virtual Security Devices Powered Off within Short Period of Time on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Cloud, BB:DeviceDefinition: Virtualization
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Virtual Machine Stopped
AND when any of Machine Identifier (custom) are contained in any of Security Devices - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND when at least 3 events are seen with the same Event Name, Username and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 45 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Multiple Virtual Security Devices Powered Off within Short Period of Time
Event description: Detects multiple virtual security devices (e.g virtual IDS, virtual SIEM component) powered off in a short period of time.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
